title: campanula garganica subsp. acarnanica
intro: Campanula garganica subsp. acarnanica is a subspecies of the Campanula garganica plant, commonly known as the 'Carpathian harebell' or 'Turkish bellflower', a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is also valued for its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Campanula garganica subsp. acarnanica can be used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
